methodology: 65% preseason projections + 35% Week 1 roto-style category performance (rank in each of 14 categories, summed). After one week, projections still dominate, but real results are starting to matter.

rankings.blurb: The preseason #2 takes the crown after Week 1, and frankly nobody should be surprised. 22 runs, 7 dingers, 21 RBI, and a .478 SLG — the bats showed up like they had a personal vendetta against baseballs. The pitching? Only 11 strikeouts, zero wins, zero quality starts — but who needs pitching when your lineup hits like it's on a mission from God? The 7-5-4 win over Nine Spoons was comfortable. A roto rank of 6th feels harsh given the offensive explosion, but when your staff posts a 4.50 ERA with a 1.50 WHIP and 2.20 K/BB, the pitching categories drag you down. The projections still love this roster, and one more week of Bobby Witt and Lindor doing their thing should cement the top spot.

Wait...wait...wait...wait — this team LOST their matchup 12-2-2 to Señor Burns and rockets up to #2 in the power rankings? That's the beauty of roto-style analysis, baby. A 2.36 ERA, 1.01 WHIP, 51 Ks, 3 wins, 8 homers, and a .448 SLG says this team is legit regardless of who they drew in Week 1. They just happened to run into the buzzsaw that was Señor Burns' historic week. The 1 stolen base is a problem, and 17 RBI from a lineup hitting 8 homers suggests some unfortunate sequencing. The roto numbers say 2nd-best team in the league. The projections had them 5th. The blend says #2. Sometimes you lose the battle but the spreadsheet says you're winning the war.

Excellent. 27 runs. 10 homers. 27 RBI. A .318 AVG. A .441 OBP. A .568 SLG. A 2.36 ERA and 0.98 WHIP. A 12-2-2 demolition of Caminero Crush. This team ranked #1 in roto across all 14 categories — first in R, first in HR, first in RBI, first in AVG, first in OBP, first in SLG. That's six first-place finishes on offense alone. So why only #3 overall? Because the preseason projections had them at 6th, and one week of dominance only gets 35% weight in the blend. The projections still worry about the speed (5 SB was nice but unsustainable) and long-term run production. But if Burns puts up another week like this, the throne is theirs. Release the hounds.

The team that was projected 0-11 against the field in preseason just won their first matchup 8-5-3 over The Polar Express. Redemption indeed. 53 strikeouts led the entire league. 5 saves+holds — also league-best. 3 wins tied for tops. The pitching staff carried this team on its back like a sherpa up Everest. The problem is the offense — 14 runs, 1 homer, 14 RBI, and a .348 SLG is the kind of production that makes you wonder if the hitters are using baguettes instead of bats. The roto numbers say 7th, the preseason projections said 3rd, and the blend lands them at 4th. Pitching keeps you in games; hitting wins them. ShawSwank is still figuring out which side of that equation they want to be on.

Wait...wait...wait...wait — the preseason #1 team posted an 8.03 ERA, a 1.70 WHIP, and LOST to Duran Duran? Judge, Soto, Harper, Freeman, and Riley combined for 13 RBI and a .225 batting average? The roto numbers say 11th out of 12, which is the kind of freefall that makes you check if the roster got hacked. The only reason they're still at #5 is the preseason projections carrying 65% of the weight — without that safety net, this team would be in the basement eating cold pizza. The 3 saves+holds and 3 steals were fine, but an 8.03 ERA is a dumpster fire wrapped in a tire fire. One bad week from the best roster on paper. Probably. Hopefully. The blend methodology is the only thing keeping this team from total embarrassment.

The pitching was supposed to be the strength — Skenes, Ohtani on the mound, Ragans — and instead they delivered a 5.59 ERA with zero wins, zero saves+holds, and 27 strikeouts. That K total is embarrassing for a staff with this pedigree. The bats actually showed some life with 7 homers and 4 steals, but a .206 AVG means a lot of those at-bats ended in existential despair. The 7-5-4 loss to Young and Yandy stings because this team had the talent to compete. Roto rank of 8th is a cold shower. The projections still believe in this roster at 4th overall, which is the only thing keeping them from freefall. If Skenes and Ohtani pitch like themselves next week, this ranking looks very different.

Lost the matchup 8-6-2 to Camp of the New Orleans Saints but posted the best ERA (2.18) and WHIP (0.91) in the entire league. Those are video game numbers. The 4.09 K/BB ratio was second-best too. So why the loss? Because 15 RBI and a .300 OBP from the offense is bringing a butter knife to a steak dinner. This team is a pitching fortress with nobody inside. The roto numbers say 5th, the preseason said 7th, and they land exactly where they started at #7. Perfectly balanced, as all mediocre things should be. If the bats ever wake up — even slightly — those pitching ratios could carry this team into the top 4.

A convincing 10-3-3 win over PO' BOYD SANDWICH, fueled by a league-second .385 OBP and a 3.02 ERA that had no business being that good. The roto numbers scream 4th-best team in the league, but the preseason projections at 8th are holding this squad back like an anchor on a speedboat. Ohtani, Acuna, Tatis, Elly — the star power is undeniable, and 3 wins from the pitching staff tied for tops. But zero saves+holds and only 1 stolen base from a team with Elly De La Cruz and Acuna? That's like having a Ferrari and driving it in a school zone. The blend methodology is punishing this team for preseason skepticism, but the roto performance says the doubters might be wrong.

rankings.team_name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints
rankings.blurb: The best K/BB ratio in the league (4.33), a .295 batting average, 25 runs scored, and 52 strikeouts from the staff. An 8-6-2 win over Balls to the Wall was earned, not lucky. The roto numbers say this is the 3rd-best team right now, but the preseason projections at 9th are an anchor dragging them to #9 in the blend. That 5.70 ERA contradicts the beautiful K/BB — meaning the pitchers are striking guys out and then getting torched in between, which is a fascinating and deeply frustrating combination. Zero saves+holds is structural, not situational. If the ERA normalizes closer to the K/BB, this team could be a legitimate top-5 contender by April. The projections just need more data to believe it.

A 10-3-3 loss to CLAUDE 4 COMMISH, and honestly it could have been worse. The pitching ratios were actually decent — a 4.04 ERA and 1.09 WHIP are respectable numbers — but the offense hit .198 with a .305 SLG. That's not hitting, that's flailing at air with mild conviction. 13 runs scored was dead last in the league. The preseason projections had concerns about the bats, and Week 1 confirmed every single one of them with a highlighter and exclamation points. The roster has arms for days (Fried, Bradish, King, Rodon, Peralta — some still benched) but swings that belong in a museum of things that don't connect. At least the 1.09 WHIP was 4th-best in the league. Silver linings on a very dark cloud.

Dead last in the roto rankings with a category sum of 119 across 14 categories. A 5.88 ERA, a 1.66 WHIP, a .279 OBP, and a .333 SLG — this team managed to be bad at almost everything simultaneously, which is actually impressive in its own depressing way. The 3.89 K/BB ratio and 3 saves+holds were the only bright spots, like finding a twenty-dollar bill in a burning building. The 8-5-3 loss to ShawSwank Redemption — a team projected to go 0-11 — is the kind of result that makes you question everything. The preseason had them at 10th, the roto says 12th, and the blend says 11th. All aboard the struggle bus. Next stop: nowhere good.

They won! They actually won! A 7-6-3 victory over the preseason #1 team Universal Basic Civale is the kind of upset that makes you believe in miracles, relief pitching, and the concept of sample size. 7 stolen bases led the league, 4 saves+holds tied for best, and 3 quality starts were solid. But a .140 batting average. Point. One. Four. Zero. A .244 OBP and .245 SLG means this team's hitters are essentially decorative furniture in the lineup. The roto rank of 9th is actually better than the preseason projection of 12th, so technically this is progress — like going from 'total disaster' to merely 'regular disaster.' The preseason rank of 12th carries 65% weight in the blend, and even a giant-killing upset can't overcome that math. Baby steps.
